rationale: Y is stronger overall because the structured Background, Case, and Conclusion format makes the same clinical sequence easier to scan and more journal-ready for an EP case abstract. Both abstracts define a specific gap: sequential SEH and DRT after LAAC with opposing antithrombotic demands. Both provide clinically relevant time points and management changes, but neither gives quantitative thrombus size, objective neurologic metrics, or a clear duration for remaining follow-up, so results rigor is moderate. Both conclusions slightly overstate what a single case can support, especially claims that procedural quality control and structured follow-up are essential to optimize net clinical benefit.
loser_weakness: X most lacked structured presentation and concise case-report framing; its single-paragraph format makes the timeline and clinical takeaways less immediately accessible.
